news.headline: Clarke steps down as Scotland boss after World Cup exit
news.desc: Steve Clarke resigns as Scotland head coach following the nation's group exit from the World Cup.

news.headline: Job done - but England will not win World Cup unless they improve
news.desc: England did their job topping the group - but they can forget winning the World Cup if they do not improve, writes Phil McNulty.

news.headline: Scotland's ill-fated World Cup story finally at a sorry end
news.desc: BBC Sport Scotland's chief sportswriter Tom English sums up his verdict on the nation's World Cup exit.

news.headline: 'Bye-bye, Scotland!' - Clarke's leaving letter to the Tartan Army
news.desc: Steve Clarke's letter to the Scotland fans in full following his departure as head coach.

news.headline: Kane becomes England's top World Cup scorer - and now chases other greats
news.desc: Harry Kane became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer with his 12th goal in the win against Panama on Saturday, but how does he compare to other country's greats?

news.headline: Tuchel worried about right-back situation
news.desc: Thomas Tuchel says he is "worried" about England's right-back crisis after Jarell Quansah became the latest in the position to suffer injury.

news.headline: Who had the X-factor and who had a tough game? England player ratings
news.desc: BBC Sport's England reporter Alex Howell rates the players after the Three Lions'  win over Panama in the World Cup.

news.headline: Gakpo asks for privacy after loss of unborn son
news.desc: Noa van der Bij, the partner of Liverpool and Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo, announces the pair have lost their unborn son.

news.headline: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
news.desc: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

news.headline: What each team needs to reach World Cup knockouts
news.desc: With the final round of group fixtures nearing a conclusion the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

news.headline: Spain's Pino may miss rest of World Cup
news.desc: Spain winger Yeremy Pino may miss the rest of the World Cup with a suspected broken collarbone.

news.headline: One night in Miami: The Scotsmen of the apocalypse
news.desc: BBC Scotland's Scott Mullen paints the scene from Miami as Scotland's World Cup hopes fade.
